resources.string: Changing the ISBN clears any cached metadata for the old one and lets you look up the new one. Continue?
Things about the book itself — edition, condition, where you got it. For a note about who you\'ve lent it to, add it when you lend the book; those notes stay in your encrypted vault.
It will be marked as removed from the library and won\'t appear on your published page. Nothing is erased — you can restore it any time, and loan history stays intact.
Pitak needs your camera to scan ISBN barcodes. Granting it stays on this device — Pitak does not send camera frames anywhere.
Camera access was permanently denied. Open Settings to grant it manually.
No metadata found for that ISBN
Pitak couldn\'t find this ISBN in Open Library or Google Books. You can search by title, save with just the ISBN, or fill the form yourself.
You may be offline, or both providers are unreachable. You can save with just the ISBN now and refresh later, or fill the form yourself.
Scan or type books you want to buy. Mark them purchased when you get them.
Would you like to add this to your Library at the same time?
This book is already in your Library. The wishlist entry was marked purchased; tap to open the existing book.
Book records are generated using Pitak, a free personal and community library software developed by Parallel Line Foundation
Choose which fields appear as columns. Title is always included.
Pitak JSON, Pitak bundle (.zip, with covers), and Goodreads CSV are supported.
Loans and borrowers are private. Tap Enable to set up your encrypted vault.
Unlock the vault to view loans, borrowers, and pending reminders.
Confirm with your fingerprint or device screen lock to set up the vault
Confirm with your fingerprint or device screen lock
Use your fingerprint or your device PIN/pattern
When you lend a book, it shows up here. Open a book from the Library and tap Lend.
No borrowers yet. They\'re created automatically the first time you lend a book to someone new.
Or create a new borrower (just type their name)
Kept in your encrypted vault with the borrower\'s details.
Pick a borrower or type a new name.
Without a backup passphrase, you can\'t restore your vault on a new phone.
Used only to encrypt portable backup files. Required to restore on a new phone. Pitak can\'t recover this for you.
Optional hint (anyone with this phone can read this)
This deletes all borrowers, loans, and the backup passphrase. Your Library and Wishlist on this device are kept. There is no undo.
Publish a public, browsable copy of your Library to GitHub Pages. Your wishlist, loans, and notes stay on this device.
After your first publish, enable Pages in your repo Settings → Pages → Deploy from a branch → main / root.
Optional. Anything you enter here is shown publicly on your published page. Leave a field blank to omit it.

Pitak uses your own GitHub OAuth App for sign-in — nothing runs on a developer server. Register one at github.com/settings/applications/new with the Device Flow option checked and paste the Client ID below.
That doesn\'t look like a Client ID. Paste just the Client ID from your OAuth App\'s settings page — not the full URL.
Waiting for you to authorize on github.com…
Pitak does not talk to Cloudflare directly. To serve your library from Cloudflare Pages in addition to GitHub Pages, do this once on your own Cloudflare account:
2. Workers & Pages → Create application → Pages → Connect to Git.
3. Pick the same GitHub repo you publish to from Pitak. Build settings: none. Output directory: /.
4. Save and deploy. Every time you publish from Pitak, Cloudflare redeploys automatically.
Bundle your books, wishlist, vault, and the passphrase blob into a single encrypted archive you can save anywhere — Drive, Dropbox, an SD card, your laptop.
No backup yet on this device.
Your last backup is older than %1$d days. If you lose this phone you lose your vault.
This replaces all books, wishlist, and vault data on this device. There is no undo.
Pitak will close so the new data takes effect. Re-open the app from your launcher.
Your books and wishlist are stored unencrypted on this device. They\'re the data you can export, publish, and share.
Borrowers and loan records live in an encrypted vault, unlocked with your biometric. They\'re never published and never leave the device except inside an encrypted backup.
Bring in books from a Pitak export (.json), a Pitak bundle (.zip, with covers), or a Goodreads CSV.
Save your books and wishlist as JSON, CSV, a bundle with cover images, or PDF.
Your Library and Wishlist. Plain and shareable — no borrower or loan data.
An encrypted archive of everything — books, wishlist, and your locked vault. Needed to move to a new phone.
Share a book list with other maintainers. Each library has an ID — only files from the same library merge automatically.
Stamped on books you add, so others can see who catalogued what. Optional.
Point your camera at another maintainer\'s library QR to pair with their library.
Point at another Pitak app\'s library QR
Paired — your app now shares their library ID. Files from that library will merge automatically.
Pitak needs your camera to scan another maintainer\'s library QR and pair with their library. Granting it stays on this device — Pitak does not send camera frames anywhere.
Have another maintainer scan this to pair their app with your library.

This detaches your app from the current library. Your books stay, but files you export will no longer merge automatically with maintainers who still use the old ID. You can re-pair by scanning their QR.
Pick a library file another maintainer shared with you. New books are added; anything that differs is shown for you to decide.
This file is from “%1$s”, a different library than yours (“%2$s”). How do you want to bring it in?
This file doesn\'t carry a library ID, so it may be from a different library than yours. How do you want to bring it in?
Join — keep both, combine the lists
This removes all your current books and replaces them with the ones in this file. This can\'t be undone. Use Join instead if you want to keep your books.
Create or restore an encrypted backup archive (includes the vault, protected by your backup passphrase).
Protects your encrypted backup archives so they can be restored on another device. Forgetting it only affects old backups, not the data on this phone.
Require a PIN (and optionally your biometric) every time the app opens or returns after being away. This gates the whole app; it\'s separate from the vault that protects borrower data.
Use your fingerprint or face to unlock, with your PIN as a fallback.
PIN must be at least 4 digits.
Confirm your phone\'s screen lock to turn off app lock. Your library and vault data are not affected.
Too many attempts. Try again in %1$d s.
Shown on the home tab and as the heading of your published page. Leave blank to use the default \"Library\".
Optional. Your library name in your own language/script — shown as a second line on the welcome screen. Leave blank to show only the English name.
Android 12+ only. Replaces the saffron palette with one derived from your wallpaper.
पिटक — your personal library, on your device.
Free to use, provided “as is” with no warranty. You are responsible for your own data, backups, and anything you publish. We collect nothing and are not liable for any loss. Use at your own risk.
Your last backup was more than %1$d days ago. Open Backup to refresh it.
No bookmarks yet. Add a link to someone else\'s published library to open it here.
Enter a name and a valid https:// link.
Unlock the vault to delete a book with loan history.
Couldn\'t prepare the export to share.
A book with this ISBN already exists in your library. What would you like to do?
No metadata found for that ISBN.
This ISBN is already on your wishlist. You can open the existing entry instead.
GitHub is building your page — it may take a minute to refresh.
GitHub Pages reported a build error. Check the repo\'s Pages settings.
Availability wasn\'t included because the vault was locked. Unlock the vault before publishing to show Available / All-out status on your page.
Archive is from a newer Pitak (v%1$d).
When on, long-press any UI text in Pitak to suggest a better translation. Suggestions go to a public GitHub repo. Turn off any time.
Draw a box around every UI text you can long-press to suggest a translation. Stays on until you turn it off.
Pitak is translated by its community. If you can read another language, you can suggest better wording — right from the app.
Create a free GitHub account (this is where suggestions are collected).
Turn on Contributor mode using the switch above.
Long-press any text in the app you\'d like to translate.
Type your suggestion and submit — it opens a pre-filled form for you.
No browser found to open the link.
No app available to open GitHub.
A crash report is saved on your phone. Send it to help the developer fix this, or dismiss it.

Reports are kept on your phone until you send or delete them. Tap Send to open a prefilled GitHub Issue Form in your browser; the full trace is copied to your clipboard so you can paste it as a comment after submitting.
No crash reports — nothing to send right now.
No browser available to open the report.
Your suggestion opens on GitHub, where it\'s posted publicly — like a public comment. Anyone can see your GitHub username, the original text, and your translation.

The crash report opens on GitHub, posted publicly like a comment under your GitHub username. It includes your phone model, Android version, and language setting, plus where the app crashed — but nothing you typed into your library.
No GitHub account? You can create one in a moment, or copy this and share it another way.
Opens GitHub — posts publicly under your GitHub name.

GitHub is a free, widely used site for sharing and improving software. Creating an account takes a minute. The name you choose becomes your public handle — it shows on anything you post, similar to a username on any social site.

Whatever name you\'re signed in with on GitHub is the name that will show publicly. You can change your GitHub username in GitHub\'s settings if you\'d prefer a different one — note that changing it updates your old links and can affect other projects you\'ve contributed to, so it\'s worth choosing one you\'re happy to keep.
